analysis.period_summary: For the week of 2026-04-27 to 2026-05-03, the funnel generated $7,311.40 USD net revenue (ClickBank) across 171 frontend sales, with backend upsells adding a further $3,163.16 in gross backend revenue. The single most critical finding is the checkout-to-purchase rate collapse on the high-volume /d/ landing flows: /d/ → /offer/v4-v2 converts at only 5.43% and /d/ → /offer/v4/ at 8.05%, meaning over 1,400 checkout clicks from the biggest traffic source are converting well below the 15% minimum target, severely suppressing overall revenue.

funnel_scorecard.commentary: The funnel top and middle perform well — landing-to-optin rates are healthy (22–33%) and offer-to-checkout rates are strong (85–91%) across primary flows — but the checkout-to-purchase step is the dominant leak, especially on the highest-volume /d/ landing traffic. Backend monetization is functional but OTO2 take rate at 9.4% is critically below benchmark.

funnel_leaks.why_it_matters: With 718 checkout clicks converting at only 5.43%, raising this to the 15% target on the highest-volume flow alone would add ~68 additional sales (~$2,516 revenue) per week at current traffic.

Only 1 purchase from 218 checkout clicks is a near-zero conversion rate suggesting a broken or mismatched checkout flow for the /e/ landing → /offer/v4/ path, likely a traffic-offer mismatch or tracking/redirect error.

197 checkout clicks yielding only 5 purchases on the /e/ → /offer/v4-v2 path indicates the /e/ landing page audience is poorly qualified relative to the offer, destroying revenue potential from a 2,556-view campaign.
OTO2 Timeline at 9.4% take rate is critically below the 15% minimum benchmark; improving to 15% would add ~9 more OTO2 sales (~$423 net) per week without any additional traffic.

The /offer/ (non-versioned) path shows 238 offer page views but only 2 checkout clicks, suggesting a broken CTA or redirect misconfiguration that is wasting residual traffic landing on the old offer URL.

upsell_analysis.commentary: OTO1 at 27.5% is in the average band but has headroom to reach the 40% strong threshold — the low OTO1 downsell acceptance (2.9%) suggests the downsell page may be underperforming or poorly positioned. OTO2 at 9.4% is critically weak; combined with 13 downsell conversions (7.6%), the Timeline product is generating most of its backend revenue via the discounted path, which warrants a review of the OTO2 VSL or pricing anchor.

needle_movers.why: The /d/ landing page drives the two highest-volume flows (3,367 + 3,518 views) but checkout-to-purchase rates of 8.05% and 5.43% are critically below the 15% floor. Contrast this with /d-v3/ achieving 15.65% and 18.64% on similar offers — confirming the issue is specific to the /d/ variant audience or a segment mismatch, not the offer itself.
what: Audit the /d/ landing page copy and traffic source targeting to identify why its audience is less purchase-ready than /d-v3/ despite similar optin rates
A/B test the /d/ landing page with the /d-v3/ copy or structure to attempt to lift checkout conversion to parity (~15%)
Consider temporarily reducing ad spend on the /d/ path and reallocating budget to /d-v3/ while testing, to protect revenue
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Lifting /d/ → /offer/v4/ from 8.05% to 15% on 696 checkout clicks = ~48 additional sales × $37 AOV = ~$1,776 incremental frontend revenue per week; /d/ → /offer/v4-v2 from 5.43% to 15% on 718 clicks = ~69 additional sales = ~$2,553 per week.

needle_movers.why: Campaign 118 (/e/ landing, 2,556 views) generated only 6 total CPV conversions and a 0.46% checkout-to-purchase on v4 and 2.54% on v4-v2 — both critically below benchmark and dramatically underperforming all other flows. This is the weakest campaign by conversion rate and is likely a traffic quality or audience-offer mismatch issue.
what: Pause or sharply reduce campaign 118 traffic immediately to stop burning checkout clicks at near-zero purchase rate
Investigate the /e/ audience segment — compare demographics, interests, or email list quality versus /d-v3/ which performs well
If retesting /e/, route to /d-v3/ landing page copy first to isolate whether the landing page or the audience segment is the root cause
needle_movers.revenue_impact: 415 checkout clicks at 0.46–2.54% vs. a 15% target represents ~51–60 lost sales per week (~$1,887–$2,220 in unrealised frontend revenue) — fixing or pausing this flow has immediate ROI.

needle_movers.area: OTO2 Timeline take rate — below critical threshold
needle_movers.why: OTO2 Timeline take rate of 9.4% is critically below the 15% minimum, and the OTO2 downsell is absorbing 13 of the 29 total OTO2 conversions (44.8% of OTO2 revenue comes via the discounted path) — signalling buyers are rejecting the full-price offer but still want the product at a lower price.
what: Revise the OTO2 VSL or sales copy to strengthen the value proposition and urgency before the price is revealed
Test increasing the OTO2 downsell price from $37 to $42 to capture more value from the high downsell acceptance rate
Add social proof, testimonials, or a guarantee specific to the Timeline product to address purchase hesitation at full price
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Lifting OTO2 from 9.4% to 15% on 171 buyers = ~9 additional full-price OTO2 sales × $47 = ~$423/week; shifting 5 of the current downsell buyers to full price adds another ~$50 — combined ~$470/week backend uplift.

needle_movers.area: OTO1 Blueprint take rate — below strong threshold
needle_movers.why: OTO1 at 27.5% is average but 12.5 percentage points below the 40% strong benchmark; with 171 frontend buyers, reaching 40% would add ~21 more OTO1 sales, and the extremely low OTO1 downsell acceptance (2.9%, only 5 sales) suggests the downsell page is not effectively catching buyers who rejected the full-price OTO1.
what: Test a stronger OTO1 page hook and headline — lead with the #1 outcome/transformation the Blueprint delivers rather than product features
Revise the OTO1 downsell page with more compelling urgency copy and a clearer value differentiation from the full-price offer
Add a video or animated demonstration of the Blueprint value on the OTO1 page to increase perceived value
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Lifting OTO1 from 27.5% to 35% on 171 buyers = ~13 additional sales × $67 = ~$871/week; improving downsell from 2.9% to 8% = ~9 more downsell sales × $47 = ~$423/week.

needle_movers.area: Scale /d-v3/ landing variant — strongest converting flow
needle_movers.why: /d-v3/ → /offer/v4-v2 delivers 32.99% optin rate, 91.47% offer-to-checkout, and 18.64% checkout-to-purchase — the best performing complete funnel path this week — yet it received only 391 views versus 3,518 for /d/, suggesting significant headroom to reallocate budget.
what: Increase traffic allocation to /d-v3/ (campaign 109) progressively while monitoring CPL and checkout-to-purchase stability
Ensure campaign 109 is not capacity-constrained by list size — if email list segment is exhausted, explore expanding to a similar audience
Run /d-v3/ alongside /d/ in a deliberate volume test to confirm performance differential holds at higher traffic volumes before full budget shift
needle_movers.revenue_impact: If /d-v3/ → /offer/v4-v2 were to receive the same 3,518 views as /d/ and maintain its 5.63% overall rate, it would generate ~198 sales vs. the ~39 currently produced by /d/ → /offer/v4-v2, a theoretical uplift of ~159 sales × $37 = ~$5,883/week in frontend revenue alone.

cpv_cb_discrepancy.note: CPV records 181 conversions vs. ClickBank's 171 frontend sales — a 10-unit gap within expected range, as CPV conversion tracking includes OTO postbacks which inflate the CPV conversion count beyond pure frontend sales.

analysis.data_notes: 1) The 'landing→offer' flow (non-versioned /offer/ path) shows a severe anomaly: 4 landing page views but 238 offer page views, suggesting direct or referral traffic is landing on the offer page without passing through the tracked landing page — this inflates offer page view counts for this flow and makes the 0.84% offer-to-checkout rate unreliable as a conversion metric. 2) GA4 funnel_v4 shows landing_to_optin_page_pct of 7720.88%, which is clearly a data error (likely a denominator mismatch or misconfigured event scope in GA4) — this GA4 landing metric should be disregarded; CPV Labs figures are used exclusively for all funnel rate calculations. 3) GA4 optin_page_to_submit_pct of 43.07% for funnel_v4 is directionally healthy (above the 30% minimum) and is noted as a supplementary signal only. 4) The /f/ landing flows (campaigns 113, f→offer_v4 and f→offer_v4_v2) recorded 29 and 25 checkout clicks respectively with zero purchases — campaign 113 is marked 'dead' in CPV; while sample size is modest, the zero-purchase outcome across 54 combined checkout clicks is anomalous and warrants investigation into whether ClickBank checkout postbacks are correctly attributed to these flows. 5) ClickBank variant_sales shows 'funnel_v4' with 38 sales and 'other' with 133 sales, but the 'other' category is not broken down further — this limits per-flow revenue attribution and means revenue figures for most funnel_totals flows are unavailable from ClickBank data.
